prs.title: Fix terminal crash when agent sends commands to busy terminals
prs.body:
Fixes #280918

## Problem
VS Code crashes when an agent attempts to send commands to a terminal that is currently running an interactive program (e.g., ncdu, vim, htop, etc.). This occurs because the execute strategies don't check if the terminal is busy before attempting to send commands.

## Solution
Added a check for `hasChildProcesses` before sending commands in all three execute strategies:
- `BasicExecuteStrategy` (basic shell integration)
- `NoneExecuteStrategy` (no shell integration)
- `RichExecuteStrategy` (rich shell integration)

When a terminal has running child processes, the strategies now throw a descriptive error instead of attempting to send commands, which prevents VS Code from crashing.

## Changes Made

### Modified Files
1. **basicExecuteStrategy.ts**
   - Added `hasChildProcesses` check before `sendText()` call
   - Throws error with clear message when terminal is busy

2. **noneExecuteStrategy.ts**
   - Added `hasChildProcesses` check before `sendText()` call
   - Consistent error handling with basicExecuteStrategy

3. **richExecuteStrategy.ts**
   - Added `hasChildProcesses` check before `runCommand()` call
   - Ensures all execute strategies have consistent behavior

### Error Message
When a terminal is busy, agents now receive:
```
Cannot execute command: terminal is currently busy with a running process. Please wait for the current process to complete or exit it before running new commands.
```

## Testing
The fix prevents crashes by gracefully handling the busy terminal state. Users should:
1. Open a terminal and run an interactive program (e.g., `ncdu`, `vim`)
2. Attempt to execute a command via agent
3. Verify that an error is thrown instead of VS Code crashing
4. Exit the interactive program and try again
5. Verify that commands execute successfully when terminal is not busy

## Impact
- **Before**: VS Code crashes when agents send commands to busy terminals
- **After**: Agents receive a clear error message, VS Code remains stable
- **Breaking Changes**: None - this only adds defensive checks
- **User Experience**: Improved stability and clearer error messages

<!-- Thank you for submitting a Pull Request. Please:
* Read our Pull Request guidelines:
  https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/How-to-Contribute#pull-requests
* Associate an issue with the Pull Request.
* Ensure that the code is up-to-date with the `main` branch.
* Include a description of the proposed changes and how to test them.
-->

prs.title: blackjack game added
prs.body: Added a fully functional Blackjack card game to the projects collection. Players can compete against a computer dealer with realistic gameplay, betting system, card animations, and theme support. Key Features: Complete Blackjack game logic with proper card values and Ace handling (1 or 11) Betting system starting with $1000 chips Smooth card-dealing animations with sound effects Dark/Light theme toggle with persistent preference Win/Loss/Draw tracking Fully responsive design for mobile and desktop Keyboard shortcuts (H for Hit, S for Stand) Checklist Ran locally without console errors Focused scope - Single game addition Updated docs or TODO comments if needed - Updated data/projects.json Files Changed data/projects.json - Added Blackjack entry projects/blackjack/index.html - Game HTML structure projects/blackjack/style.css - Complete styling with dark/light theme projects/blackjack/main.js - Full game logic and interactions Screenshots

prs.title: Feature/quiz api
prs.body: Summary This PR implements Issue #56 by replacing the static quiz questions with a dynamic fetch from the Open Trivia Database API. Key changes: Replaced the hardcoded data array in projects/quiz/main.js with questions fetched from https://opentdb.com/api.php?amount=5&type=multiple. Maintains the timer for each question, score tracking, and question rendering logic. Handles correct/wrong answers and timeout scenarios. Minor updates to data/projects.json to register the quiz project. Checklist Linked issue (#56) Ran locally without console errors Focused scope (quiz API integration only) Updated data/projects.json Screenshots Before: static questions with hardcoded array After: dynamically loaded quiz questions from API

prs.title: Feat: Use Open Trivia API for quiz questions (#56)
prs.body:
Summary

This PR implements Issue #56 by replacing the static quiz questions with a dynamic fetch from the Open Trivia Database API.
Key changes:

Replaced the hardcoded data array in projects/quiz/main.js with questions fetched from https://opentdb.com/api.php?amount=5&type=multiple.

Maintains the timer for each question, score tracking, and question rendering logic.

Handles correct/wrong answers and timeout scenarios.

Minor updates to data/projects.json to register the quiz project.

Checklist

 Linked issue (#56)

 Ran locally without console errors

 Focused scope (quiz API integration only)

 Updated data/projects.json

prs.title: solved
prs.body:
Description

This pull request adds a new keypad combinations problem (classic T9 text prediction style) along with its solution. The problem statement describes generating all possible words from a sequence of digits entered on a mobile keypad, where each digit maps to specific characters (2 → "abc", 3 → "def", etc.). The solution is implemented using recursion and backtracking to explore all possible letter combinations.

Fixes: #<issue_number>

Type of Change

 Question Added

 Solution Added

 Other (please specify):

Checklist

 I have performed a self-review of my code.

 I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas.

 I have made corresponding changes to the documentation (if applicable).

 My changes generate no new warnings.

 I have added tests to cover my changes (if applicable).

 All new and existing tests pass.

Additional Notes

The implemented solution uses backtracking with a recursive helper to generate all valid letter combinations.

The time complexity is O(4^n), where n is the length of the digit array (worst case when digits are 7 or 9).

Space complexity is O(n) for recursion depth.

Example test cases included:

Input: [2, 3] → Output: ["ad","ae","af","bd","be","bf","cd","ce","cf"]

Input: [2] → Output: ["a","b","c"]

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Introduced authentication and role-based access control, including support for Google OAuth and email/password sign-in.
  * Added protected routes for both "student" and "club" roles, with automatic redirection based on user role.
  * Implemented an authentication callback page for post-login handling and role assignment.
  * Added profile management with sign-out functionality.
  * Added new navigation links for campus events and club dashboard.

* **Refactor**
  * Updated routing structure to clearly separate public, authenticated, and role-specific routes.
  * Enhanced authentication flow and context integration across the app.
  * Replaced standalone buttons with navigation links for improved routing consistency.
  * Removed obsolete Events page component.

* **Style**
  * Restyled authentication and profile pages for improved visual hierarchy, interactivity, and responsiveness.
  * Added new button styles and loading indicators.
  * Updated CSS class naming conventions for home and campus events pages.
  * Adjusted layout positioning for floating stats element.

* **Chores**
  * Updated dependencies and metadata in the project configuration for authentication and backend integration.
  * Corrected environment variable naming for Supabase client configuration.
